title: Search a 2D Matrix
prompt: Given an m x n integer matrix where every row is sorted in non-decreasing order and the first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row, return true if `target` is in the matrix.
hints: The matrix behaves like a single sorted array of length m*n.
Map a flat index k to (k / n, k % n) and binary search.

optimal.approach: Binary search treating the matrix as a sorted 1D array via index arithmetic.

pitfalls: Wrong index math when mapping mid to (row, col).
followups: LC 240 — rows and columns sorted but not globally chained.
